questions.question: What is the purpose of a two-way frequency table in sports statistics?
A basketball team won $8$ games out of $12$ when their best player was on the court. What is the relative frequency of winning with the best player?
A two-way table shows the number of athletes who participated in a marathon, categorized by age group and finishing status. What can be inferred from the table?

options.C: The association between age group and finishing status

questions.question: A sports analyst created a two-way table to compare the performance of two tennis players, $A$ and $B$, based on the surface they played on. Which of the following can be calculated from the table?

options.A: The number of games won by player $A$ on clay courts
options.B: The relative frequency of player $B$ winning on hard courts
options.C: The association between player performance and surface type
options.D: The total number of games played by both players
options.E: The probability of player $A$ winning on grass courts

questions.question: A cycling team recorded the distance covered by their riders, categorized by the type of bike used. Which of the following is an example of a two-way table?

options.B: Type of bike vs. distance covered
options.D: Type of bike vs. rider experience

questions.question: A two-way table shows the number of soccer players who scored goals, categorized by their position on the field. What can be calculated from the table?

options.A: The total number of goals scored
options.B: The relative frequency of goals scored by each position
options.C: The association between position and goal-scoring ability
options.D: The average number of goals scored per position
options.E: The probability of a player scoring a goal

questions.question: A swimmer competed in $15$ events, winning $9$ of them. What is the relative frequency of winning?

A two-way table shows the number of athletes who participated in a triathlon, categorized by their age group and finishing status. What is the purpose of calculating relative frequencies from the table?

options.A: To compare the number of participants in each age group
options.B: To identify the most common finishing status
options.C: To analyze the association between age group and finishing status
options.D: To calculate the total number of participants

questions.question: Create a two-way table to compare the performance of two runners, $X$ and $Y$, based on the distance they ran. Calculate the relative frequencies and analyze the association between runner performance and distance.

A volleyball team recorded the number of points scored, categorized by the type of serve used. Create a two-way table and calculate the relative frequencies to analyze the association between serve type and points scored.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of two-way tables before administering the test.
Tip 2: Remind students to read each question carefully and choose the correct answer.
Tip 3: Allow students to use calculators to calculate relative frequencies.
